acceptance_targets: [CH17_AWARD_01] In the revised reveal anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, a cold reader can point to at least one concrete phenomenon behind the wall that is not just a label like wild or unclassified, and one bodily effect it has on Maren before the passage explains the containment theory. If the reader's only takeaway is the abstract concept, the revision fails.

[CH17_CRAFT_01] In revised text anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:137, the reader can identify the destructive option, the sealing option, and the moment Maren actively commits to one before he collapses. If Aldric's question at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:187 still reveals the choice for the first time, the revision fails.

[CH17_CRAFT_02] In the revised aftermath anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:223, once the text has established that Maren sealed part of the fracture and will continue, the remaining scene adds at least one new operational fact or concrete cost before the mission handoff. If the same wall, dam, maintenance, or filing-system idea is paraphrased across multiple later beats without new consequence, the revision fails.

[CH17_DIALOGUE_01] In revised dialogue across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:211, Maren does not get a standalone quotable thesis line, and he visibly revises or breaks off at least once while talking about his own role. At least half of his lines in this exchange should be grounded in immediate physical or practical detail rather than abstract cosmology. If the passage still reads like a post-battle speech, the revision fails.

[CH17_PROSE_01] Across the revised passages anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221, no more than two emphatic Not-based correction beats remain, and the others are replaced by distinct sentence shapes that still make the local contrast legible. If adjacent revelation beats still lean on the same binary-negation rhythm, the revision fails.
[R-08] Ch 17's bowl passages do not restate the bowl's market origin or eleven-year age. Physical details are specific to the Woundmouth scene.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised reveal anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, a cold reader can point to at least one concrete phenomenon behind the wall that is not just a label like wild or unclassified, and one bodily effect it has on Maren before the passage explains the containment theory. If the reader's only takeaway is the abstract concept, the revision fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's biggest reveal becomes explanatory before it becomes singular. Once Maren reaches the fissure, the prose quickly names the other side as wild, pre-classified reality and translates the System into dam and maintenance language, but it never gives one irreducibly specific glimpse of what that reality actually is. The beat is legible, but it reads like cosmology summary instead of a once-in-the-book encounter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the beyond-the-wall reveal centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:125 so Maren encounters one or two concrete, impossible particulars before he abstracts the System into dam and wall language. Keep the containment logic, but let the explanation around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:127 arrive as inference after sensory evidence rather than as the first rendering of the thing itself.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised text anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:137, the reader can identify the destructive option, the sealing option, and the moment Maren actively commits to one before he collapses. If Aldric's question at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:187 still reveals the choice for the first time, the revision fails.
findings.problem: The required state shift is Maren choosing maintenance over destruction, but the text does not stage destruction as a live option while he is in contact with the crack. He seals the Woundmouth first, then Aldric tells him afterward that he could have broken it. That makes the defining choice retrospective certification rather than dramatized action.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add the fork inside the consumption sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:133. While Maren is at the fissure, make the outward push versus inward sealing legible as two different actions or impulses, with an immediate cost or temptation attached to each. Then trim or reframe the aftermath exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:187 so Aldric is confirming the consequence of a choice already seen, not introducing the existence of the choice.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised aftermath anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:223, once the text has established that Maren sealed part of the fracture and will continue, the remaining scene adds at least one new operational fact or concrete cost before the mission handoff. If the same wall, dam, maintenance, or filing-system idea is paraphrased across multiple later beats without new consequence, the revision fails.
findings.problem: By the time the chapter reaches Maren being both crack and sealant, the wall-thesis has already landed. The aftermath then re-proves the same point in cleaner abstract language through the filing-system explanation, the maintenance-worker framing, and the meta comparison to how stories usually stage heroic choices. The scene keeps translating its meaning instead of turning again.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or retool the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:213. Keep the must-land beats that need to remain on page: Maren survives, he has sealed part of the wound, he commits to the work, and Aldric identifies other cracks. Any retained material in this span should introduce a new cost, limit, or operational consequence rather than restating the wall thesis in cleaner terms.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised dialogue across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:211, Maren does not get a standalone quotable thesis line, and he visibly revises or breaks off at least once while talking about his own role. At least half of his lines in this exchange should be grounded in immediate physical or practical detail rather than abstract cosmology. If the passage still reads like a post-battle speech, the revision fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's central exchange makes Maren sound too composed for both the moment and his style-bible voice profile. "Because someone has to do the maintenance" lands as an aphoristic mic-drop, the follow-up becomes a mini-speech diagnosing the world, and his self-definition arrives in finished thesis sentences. That is composed dialogue: thesis-speech, quotable line, mini-speech diagnosis, and tidy self-explanation in a moment where Maren should be shorter, rougher, and more self-interrupting under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Maren's side of the conversation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:211 so the same decision comes through pressure-true speech: immediate bodily detail from the consumption, at least one self-correction or abandoned clause, and more push-pull with Aldric instead of a single uninterrupted explanation. Preserve Aldric as an interlocutor rather than an audience for Maren's thesis.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221, no more than two emphatic Not-based correction beats remain, and the others are replaced by distinct sentence shapes that still make the local contrast legible. If adjacent revelation beats still lean on the same binary-negation rhythm, the revision fails.
findings.problem: One marked sentence scaffold recurs often enough to feel automatic: emphatic negation followed by correction or restatement. The chapter reaches for the same Not X. Not Y. rhythm in the reveal, the dying beat, the recovery, and the closing exchange, so different moments start landing with the same manufactured emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221. Keep emphasis where needed, but replace several current negation pivots with direct image, cause-and-effect syntax, or noun-first observation so each beat earns its own cadence.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17's bowl passages do not restate the bowl's market origin or eleven-year age. Physical details are specific to the Woundmouth scene.
findings.evidence: Ch 17 L4437: 'The ceramic was cold through his shirt...the same cheap market-stall bowl. Different man holding it.' L4467: 'The ceramic was white and cracked, glazed once but worn to matte by eleven years of handling.' The market-stall origin and eleven-year age are restated after 80+ prior uses of 'eleven years' and 5+ prior bowl re-inventories.
findings.problem: Ch 17's Woundmouth sequence re-describes the bowl twice, restating its market origin (L4437) and eleven-year age (L4467). By the climactic chapter, the bowl should be a familiar object that needs no biography. First established Ch 1 L9.
findings.rewrite_direction: At L4437, keep 'Different man holding it' — that is the meaningful beat. Cut 'the same cheap market-stall bowl.' At L4467, cut 'glazed once but worn to matte by eleven years of handling' and replace with sensory detail specific to this moment (the quest-light filling it, its weight, its temperature).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
